Description: Short-term demand shock due to halted flights and economic uncertainty.
China's deepening WTO integration drove rapid export growth and global supply chain shifts.
Fears of supply disruption from the Middle East pushed oil prices sharply higher.
Speculation and strong demand from China push Brent to all-time highs.
Lehman Brothers collapse triggered a massive global economic downturn, causing a sharp and sustained fall in energy demand.
Massive oil spill in the Gulf of Mexico, leading to tightened offshore drilling regulations.
Eurozone debt crisis threatened economic stability and dampened energy demand across Europe.
Political upheavals across the MENA region disrupted oil production and raised supply fears.
Armed conflict in Libya severely disrupted the country's oil production and exports, amplifying regional supply fears.
Military intervention by NATO forces escalated the conflict, extending oil supply disruptions.
European Union agreed to ban imports of Iranian crude oil, tightening global supply.
Russia annexed Crimea, leading to Western sanctions and heightened geopolitical tensions in energy markets.
Crude oil prices fell from over $100 to below $50 per barrel, impacting oil-exporting economies worldwide.
Saudi Arabia blocked production cuts to defend market share against US shale, triggering a major multi-year price crash.
Military intervention in Yemen raised fears of regional instability in the oil-rich Middle East.
Major sell-off in Chinese equities caused global market volatility and demand uncertainty.
JCPOA implementation lifted international sanctions on Iran, allowing its oil back into the global market.
UK voted to leave the EU, causing sterling depreciation and trade uncertainty that pressured energy markets.
OPEC members reached a preliminary agreement to limit crude oil production for the first time since 2008.
OPEC and non-OPEC producers formally agreed to cut oil production to reduce oversupply and stabilise prices.
Several Arab nations severed diplomatic ties with Qatar, increasing regional tensions in a key LNG producer.
Escalating tariffs between the US and China disrupted global trade flows and weakened demand expectations.

Event Name: US Withdraws from Iran Nuclear Deal
Description: The US reinstated sanctions on Iran's oil exports, removing significant supply from global markets.
Drone attacks on Saudi oil facilities temporarily knocked out over 5% of global oil supply.
US drone strike killed Iranian general Qasem Soleimani, spiking fears of a broader Middle East conflict.
Failure to agree on production cuts led Saudi Arabia and Russia to flood the market with cheap oil, sending prices crashing.
Global pandemic caused widespread economic disruption and a historic collapse in energy demand.
Unprecedented demand destruction from COVID-19 lockdowns caused US crude futures to drop below zero, reaching -$37.
Post-pandemic supply chain bottlenecks caused widespread shortages and drove price increases across commodities.

Event Name: Suez Canal Blocked by Ever Given
Description: The Ever Given container ship ran aground, blocking a vital global shipping chokepoint for six days.
Surging post-pandemic demand and supply constraints led to massive price spikes in natural gas and oil.
Full-scale invasion of Ukraine triggered sweeping Western sanctions on Russian energy exports, causing massive supply fears and price spikes.
The US announced a total ban on imports of Russian oil, gas, and coal in response to the Ukraine invasion.
Energy and food price shocks from the Ukraine war drove inflation to multi-decade highs across major economies.
EU leaders agreed to ban most imports of Russian oil, fundamentally re-routing global energy trade.

Event Name: G7 Price Cap on Russian Oil
Description: G7 nations imposed a $60/bbl price cap on Russian seaborne crude to limit Moscow's war revenues.
OPEC+ announced unexpected voluntary production cuts of over 1 million barrels per day.
Hamas surprise attack on Israel sparked a major conflict, raising fears of broader Middle Eastern escalation affecting supply routes.
Houthi attacks on commercial vessels in the Red Sea disrupted major energy trade routes, forcing ships to reroute around Africa.

Event Name: Iran Launches Drone Attack on Israel
Description: Direct confrontation between Iran and Israel sharply increased the geopolitical risk premium on oil.
Major central banks began cutting interest rates after a prolonged tightening cycle aimed at curbing inflation.
Political standoff over the central bank prompted eastern Libyan authorities to shut down the country's oil production.
Intensifying Houthi attacks on shipping in the Red Sea further disrupted global energy supply chains.

Event Name: US Policy Shift on Domestic Drilling
Description: New US administration shifted energy policy towards domestic drilling expansion and deregulation, boosting supply expectations.
Escalating attacks on tankers in key Middle Eastern chokepoints disrupted oil flows and raised insurance costs.
OPEC+ announced further production cuts in response to weakening global demand and rising non-OPEC supply.

Event Name: World Bank Warns of 2026 Oil Glut
Description: Major institutions forecast a significant oversupply of oil in 2026, putting sustained downward pressure on prices.
New rounds of strict international sanctions further isolated Iran's oil sector from global markets.
Direct military strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure severely impacted regional oil supply capabilities.
Producers extended voluntary output cuts to combat the anticipated 2026 supply glut.
Geopolitical escalation led to the closure of the world's most critical oil transit chokepoint.
